<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1" />
<title>Partial differential equations</title>
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.css">
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.js"></script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/contrib/auto-render.min.js"></script>
<script>
(function(){
  function renderAll(){
    if(typeof renderMathInElement!=='function'){ return setTimeout(renderAll,50); }
    renderMathInElement(document.body,{
      delimiters:[
        {left:'$$',right:'$$',display:true},
        {left:'$',right:'$',display:false},
        {left:'\\(',right:'\\)',display:false},
        {left:'\\[',right:'\\]',display:true}
      ],
      throwOnError:false,
      macros:{
        '\\Spec':'\\Spec',
        '\\Gal':'\\Gal',
        '\\Hom':'\\Hom',
        '\\tr':'\\tr',
        '\\ad':'\\ad',
        '\\ind':'\\ind'
      }
    });
  }
  if(document.readyState==='loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded',renderAll);
  else renderAll();
})();
</script>
<style>
  :root{
    --bg:#0f1218; --panel:#161b24; --panel2:#1c2230;
    --ink:#e8ecf1; --mute:#9aa4b2; --line:#2a3242;
    --yellow:#ffd866; --blue:#58c4dd; --green:#83c167;
    --pink:#e07a5f; --violet:#b39ddb; --cyan:#7de0d6;
  }
  *{box-sizing:border-box}
  html,body{margin:0;padding:0;background:var(--bg);color:var(--ink);
    font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,"Segoe UI",Inter,Roboto,sans-serif;
    font-size:17px;line-height:1.65}
  a{color:var(--blue)}
  code{font-family:ui-monospace,SFMono-Regular,Menlo,Consolas,monospace;
    background:#0b0f16;padding:1px 6px;border-radius:4px;color:#f0c987;font-size:0.92em}
  main{max-width:880px;margin:0 auto;padding:4rem 1.5rem 8rem}
  h1{font-size:2.4rem;letter-spacing:-0.01em;margin:0 0 0.4rem}
  h2{font-size:1.6rem;margin:3.2rem 0 0.8rem;color:#fff;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding-bottom:.4rem}
  h3{font-size:1.15rem;margin:1.8rem 0 .6rem;color:var(--yellow)}
  p{margin:0.7rem 0}
  .sub{color:var(--mute);margin-top:0;margin-bottom:2rem}
  .widget{background:var(--panel);border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:10px;
    padding:1rem 1.1rem 1.2rem;margin:1.2rem 0}
  .widget .hd{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:baseline;margin-bottom:.6rem;gap:.8rem;flex-wrap:wrap}
  .widget .hd .ttl{font-weight:600;color:#fff;font-size:.95rem;letter-spacing:.02em;text-transform:uppercase}
  .widget .hd .hint{color:var(--mute);font-size:.85rem}
  .row{display:flex;gap:.6rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center;margin:.4rem 0}
  .row label{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem}
  input[type="text"],input[type="number"],select{
    background:#0b0f16;color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .55rem;font-family:inherit;font-size:.95rem}
  input[type="range"]{accent-color:var(--yellow);flex:1;max-width:260px}
  button{background:var(--panel2);color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .7rem;cursor:pointer;font:inherit}
  button:hover{background:#242c3e}
  button.active{background:var(--yellow);color:#111;border-color:var(--yellow)}
  .readout{background:#0b0f16;border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:6px;
    padding:.6rem .8rem;font-family:ui-monospace,monospace;font-size:.92rem;color:#cbd5e1;
    min-height:1.6em;white-space:pre-wrap;word-break:break-word}
  .pill{display:inline-block;background:var(--panel2);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:999px;padding:1px 8px;font-size:.82rem;color:var(--mute);margin-right:4px}
  .note{border-left:3px solid var(--violet);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d8d2ef;background:#1a1828;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .ok{border-left:3px solid var(--green);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d5ebc8;background:#1a2418;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .bad{border-left:3px solid var(--pink);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#f4d2c7;background:#2a1a18;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  svg{display:block;margin:0 auto;max-width:100%}
  svg text{fill:var(--ink);font-family:inherit}
  nav.toc{position:sticky;top:0;background:rgba(15,18,24,.93);backdrop-filter:blur(8px);
    border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding:.6rem 1rem;z-index:10;
    display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:.9rem}
  nav.toc a{color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none}
  nav.toc a:hover{color:var(--yellow)}
  /* sticky sidebar TOC (wide screens only; narrow screens fall back to top-nav) */
  aside.sidetoc{position:fixed;top:4.2rem;
    left:max(1rem, calc(50% - 440px - 220px));
    width:200px;max-height:calc(100vh - 6rem);overflow-y:auto;
    padding-right:1rem;border-right:1px solid var(--line);
    font-size:.88rem;z-index:5}
  aside.sidetoc .sidetoc-title{font-size:.7rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute);margin:0 0 .5rem;padding-bottom:.35rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line)}
  aside.sidetoc ol{list-style:none;padding:0;margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc li{margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc a{display:block;padding:.3rem .5rem;color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none;
    border-left:2px solid transparent;line-height:1.35;
    transition:color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease}
  aside.sidetoc a:hover{color:var(--ink)}
  aside.sidetoc a.active{color:var(--yellow);border-left-color:var(--yellow)}
  @media (max-width:1180px){ aside.sidetoc{display:none} }
  @media (min-width:1181px){ nav.toc a[href^="#"]{display:none} }
  .hero{padding:3rem 0 1rem}
  .small{font-size:.88rem;color:var(--mute)}
  footer{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem;text-align:center;margin-top:4rem}
  table.plain{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:.95rem;margin:.6rem 0}
  table.plain th,table.plain td{padding:.35rem .5rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);text-align:left}
  table.plain th{color:var(--mute);font-weight:500}
  /* callback-css-auto-begin */
  aside.callback{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:rgba(88,196,221,0.05);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--cyan) 45%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.callback .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.callback ul{margin:0;padding-left:1.1rem}
  aside.callback ul li{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.callback a{color:inherit}
/* callback-css-auto-end */
  details.changelog{
    margin:2rem 0 1rem;padding:.6rem .9rem;
    background:rgba(0,0,0,0.22);
    border-radius:6px;
    font-size:.85rem;
  }
  details.changelog summary{
    cursor:pointer;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;
    font-size:.7rem;color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);
  }
  details.changelog table{margin-top:.5rem;border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:.82rem}
  details.changelog td{padding:.2rem .6rem;border-bottom:1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.07);vertical-align:top}
  details.changelog td:first-child{color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);white-space:nowrap;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
  aside.related{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 6%, transparent);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 55%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.related .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.related > div{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.related .more{color:var(--mute);font-style:italic}
  aside.related a{color:inherit}
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-begin */
  html[data-hide-widgets] .widget { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-quizzes] .quiz { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-lineage] .lineage-strip { display: none !important; }
  .mv-display-toggles { display: inline-flex; gap: .6rem; align-items: center; }
  .mv-display-toggle { background: transparent; border: 1px solid var(--line, #333); border-radius: 6px; padding: 5px 10px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.05rem; line-height: 1; color: inherit; transition: background 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease; }
  .mv-display-toggle:hover { background: var(--panel, rgba(255,255,255,0.06)); border-color: var(--mute, #666); }
  .mv-display-toggle--off { opacity: 0.35; }
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-end */
</style>

1 Classification 2 Heat equation 3 Wave equation 4 Laplace equation 5 Weak solutions 6 Regularity

Partial differential equations

Three model equations — Laplace, heat, wave — set the type signature for everything else: their discriminant says whether information sits still, smooths out, or travels along characteristics. Weak solutions and Lax–Milgram turn each into a Hilbert-space problem; elliptic regularity and Sobolev embedding hand the smoothness back.

blocks.html:
1. Classification: elliptic / parabolic / hyperbolic

A second-order linear PDE in two variables, written symmetrically as

$$a(x,y)\,u_{xx} + 2b(x,y)\,u_{xy} + c(x,y)\,u_{yy} + (\text{lower order}) = 0,$$

has a single number — the discriminant $\Delta = b^2 - ac$ — that controls almost every qualitative feature of its solutions. The sign of $\Delta$ partitions linear PDE into three universes:

$\Delta$typemodelcharacteristicswell-posed data
$<0$elliptic$\Delta u = 0$none realboundary values on $\partial\Omega$
$=0$parabolic$u_t = \Delta u$one familyinitial $+$ lateral data
$>0$hyperbolic$u_{tt} = c^2\Delta u$two familiesCauchy data on a non-characteristic curve

The characteristic curves are the integral curves of $a\,dy^2 - 2b\,dx\,dy + c\,dx^2 = 0$. Real roots exist exactly when $\Delta\ge 0$; the wave equation has two of them (the light-cone slopes $\pm 1/c$), the heat equation has the degenerate single family $t=\text{const}$, and Laplace has none — which is why elliptic problems take all-around boundary data instead of evolving in any direction.

<script>
/* ============================ §1 classification widget ============================ */
(function(){
  const svg = $('#cl-svg'), aS=$('#cl-a'), bS=$('#cl-b'), cS=$('#cl-c'),
        aO=$('#cl-a-out'), bO=$('#cl-b-out'), cO=$('#cl-c-out'),
        out=$('#cl-out'), pre=$('#cl-preset');
  const W=580, H=320, padL=46, padR=20, padT=20, padB=30;
  const xmin=-3, xmax=3, ymin=-3, ymax=3;
  const sx = x => padL + (x-xmin)/(xmax-xmin)*(W-padL-padR);
  const sy = y => padT + (ymax-y)/(ymax-ymin)*(H-padT-padB);

  const presets = {
    laplace:  {a:1, b:0, c:1},
    wave:     {a:1, b:0, c:-1},
    heat:     {a:1, b:0, c:0},
    tricomi:  {a:0.5, b:0, c:1}
  };

  function render(){
    svg.innerHTML="";
    const a = parseFloat(aS.value), b = parseFloat(bS.value), c = parseFloat(cS.value);
    aO.textContent = `a = ${a.toFixed(1)}`;
    bO.textContent = `b = ${b.toFixed(1)}`;
    cO.textContent = `c = ${c.toFixed(1)}`;

    const disc = b*b - a*c;
    let label, color;
    if(disc < -1e-9){ label = "elliptic"; color = "var(--cyan)"; }
    else if(disc > 1e-9){ label = "hyperbolic"; color = "var(--pink)"; }
    else { label = "parabolic"; color = "var(--yellow)"; }

    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:padL,y1:sy(0),x2:W-padR,y2:sy(0),stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:sx(0),y1:padT,x2:sx(0),y2:H-padB,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    for(const xv of [-2,-1,1,2]){
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:sx(xv),y1:sy(0)-3,x2:sx(xv),y2:sy(0)+3,stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:sx(0)-3,y1:sy(xv),x2:sx(0)+3,y2:sy(xv),stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
    }
    // characteristic directions: a λ² - 2b λ + c = 0  (with λ = dy/dx)
    // Real characteristics need disc ≥ 0; draw lines through origin at slopes λ.
    if(disc >= -1e-9){
      const sqrtD = Math.sqrt(Math.max(0, disc));
      const lambdas = [];
      if(Math.abs(a) > 1e-9){
        lambdas.push((b + sqrtD)/a);
        if(disc > 1e-9) lambdas.push((b - sqrtD)/a);
      } else if(Math.abs(b) > 1e-9){
        // 2bλ = c → λ = c/(2b); plus the degenerate vertical line
        lambdas.push(c/(2*b));
      } else if(Math.abs(c) > 1e-9){
        // a=b=0; characteristic equation reduces to c·dx² = 0 → vertical lines (y free)
        // draw vertical "characteristic"
      }
      for(const lam of lambdas){
        if(!isFinite(lam)) continue;
        const x1=xmin, x2=xmax;
        const y1=lam*x1, y2=lam*x2;
        svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:sx(x1),y1:sy(y1),x2:sx(x2),y2:sy(y2),
          stroke:color,'stroke-width':2,'stroke-opacity':0.85}));
      }
      if(Math.abs(a) < 1e-9 && Math.abs(b) < 1e-9 && Math.abs(c) > 1e-9){
        // vertical characteristic family
        for(const xv of [-2,-1,0,1,2]){
          svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:sx(xv),y1:sy(ymin),x2:sx(xv),y2:sy(ymax),
            stroke:color,'stroke-width':1.4,'stroke-opacity':0.55}));
        }
      }
      // extra parallel lines for parabolic/heat to show the single family
      if(Math.abs(disc) <= 1e-9 && lambdas.length===1){
        const lam = lambdas[0];
        for(const off of [-2,-1,1,2]){
          const x1=xmin, x2=xmax;
          const y1=lam*x1+off, y2=lam*x2+off;
          svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:sx(x1),y1:sy(y1),x2:sx(x2),y2:sy(y2),
            stroke:color,'stroke-width':1.2,'stroke-opacity':0.45}));
        }
      } else if(disc > 1e-9){
        // hyperbolic: light-cone style fan
        for(const lam of lambdas){
          for(const off of [-1.5,-0.7,0.7,1.5]){
            const x1=xmin, x2=xmax;
            const y1=lam*x1+off, y2=lam*x2+off;
            svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:sx(x1),y1:sy(y1),x2:sx(x2),y2:sy(y2),
              stroke:color,'stroke-width':1.0,'stroke-opacity':0.32}));
          }
        }
      }
    } else {
      // elliptic: no real characteristics — draw a few level circles for flavor
      for(const r of [0.6,1.2,1.8,2.4]){
        svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:sx(0),cy:sy(0),r:(W-padL-padR)/(xmax-xmin)*r,
          fill:'none',stroke:color,'stroke-width':1.2,'stroke-opacity':0.45}));
      }
    }

    // header label
    const hd = SVG('text',{x:W-padR-10,y:padT+18,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':14,
      fill:color,'font-weight':600});
    hd.textContent = label.toUpperCase();
    svg.appendChild(hd);
    const sub = SVG('text',{x:W-padR-10,y:padT+34,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'});
    sub.textContent = `b² − ac = ${disc.toFixed(2)}`;
    svg.appendChild(sub);

    out.textContent =
      `${a.toFixed(1)}·u_xx + ${(2*b).toFixed(1)}·u_xy + ${c.toFixed(1)}·u_yy = 0`
      + `\nDiscriminant b² − ac = ${disc.toFixed(2)} → ${label}.`
      + (disc < -1e-9
          ? "\nNo real characteristics — Dirichlet data on ∂Ω is the natural problem."
          : disc > 1e-9
            ? "\nTwo real characteristic families — information travels along them at finite speed."
            : "\nOne degenerate family — diffusive smoothing in the time direction.");
  }
  pre.addEventListener('change', () => {
    const p = presets[pre.value];
    if(!p) return;
    aS.value = p.a; bS.value = p.b; cS.value = p.c;
    render();
  });
  for(const el of [aS,bS,cS]) el.addEventListener('input', render);
  render();
})();
</script>
</section>

2. Heat equation and the heat kernel

The heat equation

$$u_t = \Delta u, \qquad u(x,0) = u_0(x)$$

describes how a temperature distribution $u_0$ on $\mathbb{R}^n$ relaxes toward equilibrium. Plugging in the Gaussian ansatz $u(x,t) = (4\pi t)^{-n/2} e^{-|x|^2/4t}$ and computing both sides shows it solves $u_t=\Delta u$ for $t>0$, and as $t\to 0^+$ it concentrates at the origin: this is the heat kernel

$$K_t(x) \;=\; \frac{1}{(4\pi t)^{n/2}}\,e^{-|x|^2/4t}, \qquad K_0 = \delta_0.$$

Convolution gives the general solution: $u(x,t) = (K_t * u_0)(x)$. Three properties are immediate.

Smoothing. Even if $u_0$ is merely $L^1$ or a distribution, $u(\cdot,t)\in C^\infty(\mathbb{R}^n)$ for every $t>0$. Heat kills singularities; rough data becomes analytic instantly.

Infinite propagation speed. $K_t(x)>0$ for every $x\in\mathbb{R}^n$ and every $t>0$. A point of heat at the origin is felt — however faintly — everywhere, immediately. This is the qualitative opposite of the wave equation.

Maximum principle. On a bounded cylinder $\Omega\times[0,T]$, $\max u$ is taken on the parabolic boundary $\{t=0\}\cup(\partial\Omega\times[0,T])$ — never strictly inside, unless $u$ is constant. Heat dampens; it cannot create new hot spots.

Heat-kernel evolution from initial data
slide $t$ to watch the Gaussian widen; switch to a sum of two deltas
t = 0.05

<script>
/* ============================ §2 heat-kernel widget ============================ */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#ht-svg'), sel=$('#ht-init'), tS=$('#ht-t'), tO=$('#ht-t-out'), out=$('#ht-out');
  const W=580, H=320, padL=46, padR=20, padT=20, padB=30;
  const xmin=-4, xmax=4, ymin=0, ymax=2.2;
  const sx = x => padL + (x-xmin)/(xmax-xmin)*(W-padL-padR);
  const sy = y => padT + (ymax-y)/(ymax-ymin)*(H-padT-padB);

  function K(t,x){ return Math.exp(-x*x/(4*t))/Math.sqrt(4*Math.PI*t); }

  function eval0(kind, x, t){
    if(kind==="delta") return K(t, x);
    if(kind==="two")   return K(t, x+1) + K(t, x-1);
    if(kind==="step"){
      // u = ∫_0^∞ K_t(x-y) dy = (1/2)(1 + erf(x/(2 sqrt t)))
      return 0.5 * (1 + erf(x/(2*Math.sqrt(t))));
    }
    return 0;
  }
  // small erf approximation (Abramowitz & Stegun 7.1.26)
  function erf(x){
    const sign = x<0 ? -1 : 1;
    x = Math.abs(x);
    const a1=0.254829592,a2=-0.284496736,a3=1.421413741,a4=-1.453152027,a5=1.061405429,p=0.3275911;
    const tt = 1/(1+p*x);
    const y = 1 - (((((a5*tt + a4)*tt) + a3)*tt + a2)*tt + a1)*tt*Math.exp(-x*x);
    return sign*y;
  }

  function render(){
    svg.innerHTML="";
    const t = parseFloat(tS.value);
    tO.textContent = `t = ${t.toFixed(2)}`;
    const kind = sel.value;

    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:padL,y1:sy(0),x2:W-padR,y2:sy(0),stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:sx(0),y1:padT,x2:sx(0),y2:H-padB,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    for(const xv of [-3,-2,-1,1,2,3]){
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:sx(xv),y1:sy(0)-3,x2:sx(xv),y2:sy(0)+3,stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
      const tx=SVG('text',{x:sx(xv),y:sy(0)+16,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      tx.textContent=String(xv); svg.appendChild(tx);
    }

    // initial-data marker(s) (cyan)
    if(kind==="delta" || kind==="two"){
      const sites = kind==="delta" ? [0] : [-1,1];
      for(const s of sites){
        svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:sx(s),y1:sy(0),x2:sx(s),y2:sy(2),
          stroke:'var(--cyan)','stroke-width':2.4}));
        svg.appendChild(SVG('polygon',{
          points:`${sx(s)-6},${sy(2)} ${sx(s)+6},${sy(2)} ${sx(s)},${sy(2.18)}`,
          fill:'var(--cyan)'}));
      }
    } else if(kind==="step"){
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:sx(0),y1:sy(0),x2:sx(xmax),y2:sy(0),
        stroke:'var(--cyan)','stroke-width':2,'stroke-dasharray':'4 3'}));
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:sx(0),y1:sy(0),x2:sx(0),y2:sy(1),
        stroke:'var(--cyan)','stroke-width':2,'stroke-dasharray':'4 3'}));
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:sx(0),y1:sy(1),x2:sx(xmax),y2:sy(1),
        stroke:'var(--cyan)','stroke-width':2,'stroke-dasharray':'4 3'}));
    }

    // u(·, t) trace (yellow)
    const M=600;
    const pts=[];
    let peak=0;
    for(let i=0;i<=M;i++){
      const x = xmin + i*(xmax-xmin)/M;
      const y = eval0(kind, x, t);
      if(y>peak) peak=y;
      pts.push([sx(x), sy(Math.min(ymax-0.05, y))]);
    }
    const d = pts.map((p,i)=>`${i===0?'M':'L'}${p[0]} ${p[1]}`).join(' ');
    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d, fill:'var(--yellow)','fill-opacity':0.16,
      stroke:'var(--yellow)','stroke-width':2}));

    // legend
    const lg1 = SVG('text',{x:W-padR-10,y:padT+14,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':12,fill:'var(--cyan)'});
    lg1.textContent = "u₀ (initial)"; svg.appendChild(lg1);
    const lg2 = SVG('text',{x:W-padR-10,y:padT+30,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':12,fill:'var(--yellow)'});
    lg2.textContent = "u(·, t)"; svg.appendChild(lg2);

    const sigma = Math.sqrt(2*t);
    out.textContent =
        `K_t(x) = (4π t)^(−1/2) exp(−x²/4t)`
      + `\nGaussian width σ = √(2t) ≈ ${sigma.toFixed(3)};   peak ≈ ${peak.toFixed(3)}`
      + (kind==="delta"
          ? "\nThe initial δ₀ has spread to a Gaussian: smoothing is instantaneous."
          : kind==="two"
            ? "\nTwo Gaussians, one per spike — they merge as σ grows past their separation."
            : "\nThe step is smoothed by erf — discontinuity becomes analytic for any t > 0.");
  }
  sel.addEventListener('change', render);
  tS.addEventListener('input', render);
  render();
})();
</script>
</section>

3. Wave equation and d'Alembert

The wave equation $u_{tt} = c^2\Delta u$ is the prototype hyperbolic equation. In one space dimension it factors:

$$\partial_t^2 - c^2\partial_x^2 \;=\; (\partial_t - c\partial_x)(\partial_t + c\partial_x),$$

which says the operator annihilates anything constant along $x \pm ct$. The general solution is therefore $u(x,t) = F(x-ct) + G(x+ct)$ — a right-mover plus a left-mover. Matching the initial data $u(x,0)=f$, $u_t(x,0)=g$ produces d'Alembert's formula:

$$u(x,t) \;=\; \tfrac{1}{2}\bigl[f(x-ct) + f(x+ct)\bigr] \;+\; \frac{1}{2c}\!\int_{x-ct}^{x+ct}\!g(s)\,ds.$$

Two halves of the position datum travel in opposite directions; the velocity datum spreads as an integrated pulse between the two characteristics.

Finite speed of propagation. $u(x_0,t_0)$ depends only on data inside the segment $[x_0-ct_0, x_0+ct_0]$ — its backward light cone. A signal cannot outrun $c$.

Huygens' principle. In odd $n\ge 3$ the dependence shrinks further — to the boundary of the cone. A pulse on $\mathbb{R}^3$ has a sharp leading and trailing edge: that is why we hear discrete claps rather than infinite reverberation. In even dimensions there is a residual tail and Huygens fails.

d'Alembert: traveling-wave decomposition
scrub $t$; the yellow profile is the sum of a left and right copy
t = 0.00

<script>
/* ============================ §3 d'Alembert wave widget ============================ */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#wv-svg'), sel=$('#wv-init'), tS=$('#wv-t'), tO=$('#wv-t-out'), out=$('#wv-out');
  const W=580, H=320, padL=46, padR=20, padT=20, padB=30;
  const xmin=-4, xmax=4, ymin=-0.6, ymax=1.4;
  const sx = x => padL + (x-xmin)/(xmax-xmin)*(W-padL-padR);
  const sy = y => padT + (ymax-y)/(ymax-ymin)*(H-padT-padB);
  const c = 1;

  function f(x, kind){
    if(kind==="bump") return Math.exp(-4*x*x);
    if(kind==="rect") return Math.abs(x)<0.5 ? 1 : 0;
    if(kind==="sin")  return Math.abs(x)<0.7 ? Math.cos(Math.PI*x/(2*0.7)) : 0;
    return 0;
  }

  function render(){
    svg.innerHTML="";
    const t = parseFloat(tS.value);
    tO.textContent = `t = ${t.toFixed(2)}`;
    const kind = sel.value;

    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:padL,y1:sy(0),x2:W-padR,y2:sy(0),stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:sx(0),y1:padT,x2:sx(0),y2:H-padB,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    for(const xv of [-3,-2,-1,1,2,3]){
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:sx(xv),y1:sy(0)-3,x2:sx(xv),y2:sy(0)+3,stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
      const tx=SVG('text',{x:sx(xv),y:sy(0)+16,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      tx.textContent=String(xv); svg.appendChild(tx);
    }

    const M=600;
    const ptsR=[], ptsL=[], ptsSum=[];
    for(let i=0;i<=M;i++){
      const x = xmin + i*(xmax-xmin)/M;
      const fr = 0.5*f(x - c*t, kind);
      const fl = 0.5*f(x + c*t, kind);
      ptsR.push([sx(x), sy(fr)]);
      ptsL.push([sx(x), sy(fl)]);
      ptsSum.push([sx(x), sy(fr+fl)]);
    }
    function poly(pts){ return pts.map((p,i)=>`${i===0?'M':'L'}${p[0]} ${p[1]}`).join(' '); }

    // right-mover (cyan, dashed)
    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d:poly(ptsR), fill:'none',
      stroke:'var(--cyan)','stroke-width':1.6,'stroke-dasharray':'4 3','stroke-opacity':0.85}));
    // left-mover (pink, dashed)
    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d:poly(ptsL), fill:'none',
      stroke:'var(--pink)','stroke-width':1.6,'stroke-dasharray':'4 3','stroke-opacity':0.85}));
    // sum (yellow, solid)
    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d:poly(ptsSum), fill:'var(--yellow)','fill-opacity':0.12,
      stroke:'var(--yellow)','stroke-width':2.2}));

    // legend
    const lg1 = SVG('text',{x:W-padR-10,y:padT+14,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':12,fill:'var(--cyan)'});
    lg1.textContent = "½ f(x − ct)"; svg.appendChild(lg1);
    const lg2 = SVG('text',{x:W-padR-10,y:padT+30,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':12,fill:'var(--pink)'});
    lg2.textContent = "½ f(x + ct)"; svg.appendChild(lg2);
    const lg3 = SVG('text',{x:W-padR-10,y:padT+46,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':12,fill:'var(--yellow)'});
    lg3.textContent = "u(x, t) = sum"; svg.appendChild(lg3);

    out.textContent =
        `c = 1.   u(x, t) = ½[f(x − t) + f(x + t)]`
      + `\nLight-cone for the spectator at x = 0:  data inside [−${t.toFixed(2)}, ${t.toFixed(2)}]`
      + (t===0
          ? "\nAt t = 0 the two halves coincide: u = f."
          : "\nLeft and right copies have separated; once they no longer overlap the bump has cleanly split.");
  }
  sel.addEventListener('change', render);
  tS.addEventListener('input', render);
  render();
})();
</script>
</section>

4. Laplace equation and the Dirichlet problem

$\Delta u = 0$ is the steady-state limit of $u_t = \Delta u$: solutions are harmonic. They satisfy the mean-value property

$$u(x_0) \;=\; \frac{1}{|\partial B_r(x_0)|}\!\int_{\partial B_r(x_0)}\! u\,dS \;=\; \frac{1}{|B_r(x_0)|}\!\int_{B_r(x_0)}\! u\,dx$$

for every ball $B_r(x_0)\subset\Omega$, which forces the strong maximum principle: a non-constant harmonic function attains its extrema only on $\partial\Omega$. Consequence: the Dirichlet problem

$$\Delta u = 0 \text{ in } \Omega, \qquad u|_{\partial\Omega} = g$$

has a unique solution (subtract any two solutions; the difference is harmonic with zero boundary data, hence zero by maximum principle).

Poisson kernel on the disk

On the unit disk $\mathbb{D}\subset\mathbb{R}^2$, the unique harmonic extension of a continuous boundary value $g(\theta)$ is given by the Poisson integral

$$u(r,\theta) \;=\; \frac{1}{2\pi}\!\int_{-\pi}^{\pi}\! P_r(\theta-\phi)\, g(\phi)\,d\phi, \qquad P_r(\alpha) \;=\; \frac{1-r^2}{1-2r\cos\alpha + r^2}.$$

The kernel is non-negative, integrates to $2\pi$, and concentrates on $\alpha=0$ as $r\to 1^-$ — so $u(r,\theta)\to g(\theta)$ at every point of continuity. The Neumann problem $\partial_\nu u = g$ on $\partial\Omega$ is solvable only when $\int_{\partial\Omega} g\,dS = 0$ (a divergence-theorem compatibility condition), and is then unique up to a constant.

Dirichlet problem on the disk via the Poisson kernel
edit the boundary; watch the harmonic interior fill in

<script>
/* ============================ §4 Poisson-kernel widget ============================ */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#lp-svg'), sel=$('#lp-bdy'), out=$('#lp-out');
  const W=580, H=360;
  const cx=W/2, cy=H/2;
  const Rpix = Math.min(W,H)/2 - 28;

  function g(theta, kind){
    if(kind==="cos")  return Math.cos(theta);
    if(kind==="cos2") return Math.cos(2*theta);
    if(kind==="step") return Math.sin(theta) > 0 ? 1 : -1;
    if(kind==="hot")  return Math.exp(-6*(1 - Math.cos(theta)));
    return 0;
  }

  // numerically integrate Poisson kernel against g
  function poisson(r, theta, kind){
    const N=256;
    let s=0;
    for(let k=0;k<N;k++){
      const phi = -Math.PI + (k+0.5)*(2*Math.PI/N);
      const denom = 1 - 2*r*Math.cos(theta - phi) + r*r;
      const P = (1 - r*r)/Math.max(1e-9, denom);
      s += P * g(phi, kind);
    }
    return s/N;
  }

  // map v ∈ [-1,1] to a theme-aware heatmap: cool side blends --blue, warm side blends --pink, midpoint is --panel.
  // Uses color-mix at the SVG fill site so the palette swaps with the theme.
  function heat(v){
    v = Math.max(-1, Math.min(1, v));
    const pct = Math.round(Math.abs(v) * 100);
    const accent = v >= 0 ? 'var(--pink)' : 'var(--blue)';
    return `color-mix(in srgb, ${accent} ${pct}%, var(--panel))`;
  }

  function render(){
    svg.innerHTML="";
    const kind = sel.value;

    // sample the disk on a polar grid; draw small annular wedges
    const Nr=18, Nt=64;
    let vmax = 0;
    // first pass to find scale (the boundary already lives in a known range so this stabilizes the heat map)
    for(let i=0;i<Nt;i++){
      const theta = -Math.PI + (i+0.5)*(2*Math.PI/Nt);
      const v = Math.abs(g(theta,kind));
      if(v>vmax) vmax = v;
    }
    if(vmax < 1e-6) vmax = 1;

    for(let j=0;j<Nr;j++){
      const r1 = j/Nr, r2 = (j+1)/Nr;
      const R1 = r1*Rpix, R2 = r2*Rpix;
      for(let i=0;i<Nt;i++){
        const t1 = -Math.PI + i*(2*Math.PI/Nt);
        const t2 = -Math.PI + (i+1)*(2*Math.PI/Nt);
        const tm = (t1+t2)/2;
        const rm = (r1+r2)/2;
        const v = poisson(rm, tm, kind)/vmax;
        const x1 = cx + R1*Math.cos(t1), y1 = cy + R1*Math.sin(t1);
        const x2 = cx + R2*Math.cos(t1), y2 = cy + R2*Math.sin(t1);
        const x3 = cx + R2*Math.cos(t2), y3 = cy + R2*Math.sin(t2);
        const x4 = cx + R1*Math.cos(t2), y4 = cy + R1*Math.sin(t2);
        // approximate annular wedge as a quad (small steps so it's fine)
        svg.appendChild(SVG('polygon',{
          points:`${x1},${y1} ${x2},${y2} ${x3},${y3} ${x4},${y4}`,
          fill:heat(v), stroke:'none'}));
      }
    }

    // draw the boundary trace as a colored ring
    const Nbnd=180;
    for(let i=0;i<Nbnd;i++){
      const t1 = -Math.PI + i*(2*Math.PI/Nbnd);
      const t2 = -Math.PI + (i+1)*(2*Math.PI/Nbnd);
      const tm = (t1+t2)/2;
      const v = g(tm,kind)/vmax;
      const R1 = Rpix, R2 = Rpix+10;
      const x1 = cx + R1*Math.cos(t1), y1 = cy + R1*Math.sin(t1);
      const x2 = cx + R2*Math.cos(t1), y2 = cy + R2*Math.sin(t1);
      const x3 = cx + R2*Math.cos(t2), y3 = cy + R2*Math.sin(t2);
      const x4 = cx + R1*Math.cos(t2), y4 = cy + R1*Math.sin(t2);
      svg.appendChild(SVG('polygon',{
        points:`${x1},${y1} ${x2},${y2} ${x3},${y3} ${x4},${y4}`,
        fill:heat(v), stroke:'none'}));
    }

    // outer outline + center dot
    svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx,cy,r:Rpix,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--ink)','stroke-width':1.4}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx,cy,r:2.5,fill:'var(--yellow)'}));
    const lab = SVG('text',{x:cx, y:cy-8, 'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11, fill:'var(--mute)'});
    lab.textContent = "u(0)"; svg.appendChild(lab);

    // mean-value readout: u(0) equals boundary average
    let meanG = 0;
    const M = 720;
    for(let k=0;k<M;k++){
      const phi = -Math.PI + (k+0.5)*(2*Math.PI/M);
      meanG += g(phi, kind);
    }
    meanG /= M;
    out.textContent =
        "Mean-value property:  u(0) = (1/2π) ∫ g(φ) dφ"
      + `\nBoundary average ≈ ${meanG.toFixed(4)}     →  predicted u(0) at center.`
      + "\nPink = positive, blue = negative.  Heat-map shows the harmonic extension.";
  }
  sel.addEventListener('change', render);
  render();
})();
</script>
</section>

5. Weak solutions and Galerkin approximation

For irregular data — or simply for the natural Hilbert-space framework — we relax "$-\Delta u = f$" to its weak formulation. Multiply by a test function $v\in C_c^\infty(\Omega)$ and integrate by parts:

$$\int_\Omega \nabla u\cdot\nabla v\,dx \;=\; \int_\Omega f\,v\,dx \qquad\text{for every } v\in C_c^\infty(\Omega).$$

This is well-defined whenever $u,v\in H^1(\Omega)$ and $f\in L^2(\Omega)$. Closing $C_c^\infty$ in the $H^1$ norm gives $H^1_0(\Omega)$ — Sobolev functions with zero boundary trace, the natural home for Dirichlet conditions.

Definition (weak solution). $u\in H^1_0(\Omega)$ is a weak solution of $-\Delta u = f$ with zero Dirichlet data if

$$a(u,v) := \int_\Omega \nabla u\cdot\nabla v\,dx \;=\; \langle f, v\rangle \qquad\forall\, v\in H^1_0(\Omega).$$

The bilinear form $a$ is bounded ($|a(u,v)|\le \|u\|_{H^1}\|v\|_{H^1}$) and coercive ($a(u,u) = \|\nabla u\|_{L^2}^2 \ge C\|u\|_{H^1}^2$ by Poincaré). So existence and uniqueness will follow from Lax–Milgram in §6.

Galerkin's method

Choose a finite-dimensional subspace $V_n = \mathrm{span}(\phi_1,\ldots,\phi_n)\subset H^1_0$. Look for $u_n=\sum_j c_j\phi_j$ with

$$a(u_n,\phi_i) = \langle f,\phi_i\rangle, \qquad i=1,\ldots,n.$$

This is a linear system $A c = b$ with $A_{ij}=a(\phi_j,\phi_i)$ and $b_i=\langle f,\phi_i\rangle$. Coercivity makes $A$ positive definite, so $c$ exists uniquely. As $\bigcup V_n$ becomes dense in $H^1_0$, the discrete solutions converge: $u_n\to u$ in $H^1_0$. Picking $\phi_i$ to be hat functions on a triangulation gives the finite element method.

Test against $\phi$: classical vs. weak
a kink fails to be $C^2$ but still satisfies $a(u,\phi)=\langle f,\phi\rangle$

<script>
/* ============================ §5 weak vs classical widget ============================ */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#wk-svg'), sel=$('#wk-fn'), out=$('#wk-out');
  const W=580, H=320, padL=46, padR=20, padT=20, padB=30;
  const xmin=0, xmax=1, ymin=-0.4, ymax=0.55;
  const sx = x => padL + (x-xmin)/(xmax-xmin)*(W-padL-padR);
  const sy = y => padT + (ymax-y)/(ymax-ymin)*(H-padT-padB);

  function u(x, kind){
    if(kind==="smooth") return x*(1-x);
    if(kind==="tent")   return 0.5 - Math.abs(x - 0.5);
    if(kind==="kinky"){
      // piecewise linear: rises to 0.4 at 0.4, falls to 0.2 at 0.7, back to 0 at 1
      if(x < 0.4) return x;
      if(x < 0.7) return 0.4 - (0.4 - 0.2)*(x - 0.4)/(0.7 - 0.4);
      return 0.2*(1 - x)/(1 - 0.7);
    }
    return 0;
  }
  function uPrime(x, kind, h=1e-4){
    return (u(x+h,kind) - u(x-h,kind))/(2*h);
  }

  function describe(kind){
    if(kind==="smooth")
      return {label:"u = x(1−x)", note:"C∞ on (0,1) — both classical and weak forms agree. Classical −u″ = 2 means u solves −u″ = 2 with u(0)=u(1)=0."};
    if(kind==="tent")
      return {label:"tent: 0.5 − |x − ½|", note:"Continuous with one kink — not C¹. Distributional derivative jumps at x=½, so −u″ = 2δ_{½}. Still weakly solves a δ-source problem."};
    if(kind==="kinky")
      return {label:"piecewise-linear kinky u", note:"Not C¹: classical second derivative is undefined at each break. But ∫ u′ φ′ is finite for every φ ∈ H¹₀, so u sits in H¹₀ and pairs against test functions just fine."};
  }

  function render(){
    svg.innerHTML="";
    const kind = sel.value;
    const info = describe(kind);

    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:padL,y1:sy(0),x2:W-padR,y2:sy(0),stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:sx(0),y1:padT,x2:sx(0),y2:H-padB,stroke:'var(--line)'}));
    for(const xv of [0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1]){
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:sx(xv),y1:sy(0)-3,x2:sx(xv),y2:sy(0)+3,stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
      const t=SVG('text',{x:sx(xv),y:sy(0)+16,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      t.textContent=String(xv); svg.appendChild(t);
    }

    // u trace (yellow)
    const M=400;
    const ptsU=[], ptsUp=[];
    for(let i=0;i<=M;i++){
      const x = xmin + i*(xmax-xmin)/M;
      ptsU.push([sx(x), sy(u(x,kind))]);
      ptsUp.push([sx(x), sy(0.18*uPrime(x,kind))]);
    }
    function poly(p){ return p.map((q,i)=>`${i===0?'M':'L'}${q[0]} ${q[1]}`).join(' '); }
    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d:poly(ptsU), fill:'none', stroke:'var(--yellow)','stroke-width':2.2}));

    // test function φ (cyan): a single bump centered at 0.5
    const ptsPhi=[];
    for(let i=0;i<=M;i++){
      const x = xmin + i*(xmax-xmin)/M;
      const phi = Math.sin(Math.PI*x);
      ptsPhi.push([sx(x), sy(0.3*phi)]);
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d:poly(ptsPhi), fill:'none', stroke:'var(--cyan)','stroke-width':1.8,'stroke-dasharray':'5 3'}));

    // u' trace (green, scaled 0.18×)
    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d:poly(ptsUp), fill:'none', stroke:'var(--green)','stroke-width':1.5,'stroke-opacity':0.75}));

    // numerically compute a(u,φ) = ∫ u' φ'
    const G=2000;
    const dx=(xmax-xmin)/G;
    let Auv=0;
    for(let i=0;i<G;i++){
      const x = xmin + (i+0.5)*dx;
      const up = uPrime(x,kind);
      const pp = Math.PI*Math.cos(Math.PI*x);
      Auv += up*pp*dx;
    }
    // and ⟨f,φ⟩ for the smooth case where f=2
    let fphi=0;
    for(let i=0;i<G;i++){
      const x = xmin + (i+0.5)*dx;
      fphi += 2*Math.sin(Math.PI*x)*dx;
    }

    // legend
    const lg1 = SVG('text',{x:W-padR-10,y:padT+14,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':12,fill:'var(--yellow)'});
    lg1.textContent = info.label; svg.appendChild(lg1);
    const lg2 = SVG('text',{x:W-padR-10,y:padT+30,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':12,fill:'var(--cyan)'});
    lg2.textContent = "φ(x) = sin πx"; svg.appendChild(lg2);
    const lg3 = SVG('text',{x:W-padR-10,y:padT+46,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':12,fill:'var(--green)'});
    lg3.textContent = "u′ (scaled)"; svg.appendChild(lg3);

    out.textContent =
        `a(u, φ) = ∫₀¹ u′(x) φ′(x) dx ≈ ${Auv.toFixed(4)}`
      + `\nFor the smooth example, ⟨f, φ⟩ = ∫ 2 sin(πx) dx = 4/π ≈ ${fphi.toFixed(4)}.`
      + `\n${info.note}`;
  }
  sel.addEventListener('change', render);
  render();
})();

6. Lax–Milgram, elliptic regularity, embedding

Three theorems wrap up the existence-and-smoothness story for elliptic problems.

Lax–Milgram

Let $H$ be a Hilbert space and $a\colon H\times H\to\mathbb{R}$ a bilinear form satisfying

  • boundedness: $|a(u,v)| \le M\|u\|\,\|v\|$,
  • coercivity: $a(u,u)\ge \alpha \|u\|^2$ for some $\alpha>0$.

Then for every $\ell\in H^*$ there exists a unique $u\in H$ with $a(u,v) = \ell(v)$ for all $v\in H$, and $\|u\|\le \alpha^{-1}\|\ell\|$. When $a$ is symmetric this reduces to the Riesz representation theorem; the asymmetric case is the new content.

Applied to $H=H^1_0(\Omega)$ with $a(u,v)=\int\nabla u\cdot\nabla v$ and $\ell(v)=\int fv$: existence, uniqueness, and an a-priori estimate $\|u\|_{H^1}\le C\|f\|_{L^2}$ — all from coercivity.

Elliptic regularity

The weak solution from Lax–Milgram is in $H^1_0$; we want to know it is actually smooth when $f$ is. Interior regularity: if $-\Delta u = f$ weakly and $f\in H^k_{\mathrm{loc}}(\Omega)$, then $u\in H^{k+2}_{\mathrm{loc}}(\Omega)$. Boundary regularity: on a $C^{k+2}$ domain with appropriate boundary data, the gain extends up to $\partial\Omega$. Bootstrapping: $f\in C^\infty\Rightarrow u\in C^\infty$.

Sobolev embedding

To turn weak derivatives in $L^p$ back into pointwise smoothness, the Sobolev embedding theorem trades $kp$ orders against the dimension $n$:

regimeconditionconclusion
subcritical$kp < n$$W^{k,p}\hookrightarrow L^{p^*}$, $\;p^* = \dfrac{np}{n-kp}$
critical$kp = n$$W^{k,p}\hookrightarrow L^q$ for every $q<\infty$ (BMO at the edge)
supercritical$kp > n$$W^{k,p}\hookrightarrow C^{0,\gamma}$, $\;\gamma = k - n/p \;(\bmod 1)$

So once enough weak derivatives lie in $L^p$, weak solutions become Hölder continuous, then $C^k$. Combine with elliptic regularity: a smooth right-hand side gives a smooth weak solution, classically.

<script>
/* ============================ §6 Sobolev embedding chart ============================ */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#em-svg'), nS=$('#em-n'), kS=$('#em-k'), pS=$('#em-p'),
        nO=$('#em-n-out'), kO=$('#em-k-out'), pO=$('#em-p-out'), out=$('#em-out');
  const W=580, H=320, padL=60, padR=20, padT=30, padB=40;
  const sx = x => padL + (x/8)*(W-padL-padR);  // p axis 0..8
  const sy = y => padT + (1 - y/8)*(H-padT-padB);  // kp axis 0..8

  function render(){
    svg.innerHTML="";
    const n = parseInt(nS.value);
    const k = parseInt(kS.value);
    const p = parseFloat(pS.value);
    nO.textContent = `n = ${n}`;
    kO.textContent = `k = ${k}`;
    pO.textContent = `p = ${p.toFixed(1)}`;

    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:padL,y1:H-padB,x2:W-padR,y2:H-padB,stroke:'var(--ink)','stroke-width':1.4}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:padL,y1:padT,x2:padL,y2:H-padB,stroke:'var(--ink)','stroke-width':1.4}));
    for(let pv=1; pv<=6; pv++){
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:sx(pv),y1:H-padB,x2:sx(pv),y2:H-padB+5,stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
      const t=SVG('text',{x:sx(pv),y:H-padB+18,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      t.textContent=String(pv); svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    for(let kpv=1; kpv<=7; kpv++){
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:padL-5,y1:sy(kpv),x2:padL,y2:sy(kpv),stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
      const t=SVG('text',{x:padL-8,y:sy(kpv)+4,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':11,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      t.textContent=String(kpv); svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    const xlab = SVG('text',{x:W-padR,y:H-padB+34,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'});
    xlab.textContent="exponent p";
    svg.appendChild(xlab);
    const ylab = SVG('text',{x:padL-50,y:padT-6,'font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'});
    ylab.textContent="kp";
    svg.appendChild(ylab);

    // critical horizontal line kp = n (bold cyan)
    const yCrit = sy(n);
    if(yCrit > padT && yCrit < H-padB){
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:padL,y1:yCrit,x2:W-padR,y2:yCrit,
        stroke:'var(--cyan)','stroke-width':2,'stroke-dasharray':'6 4'}));
      const t = SVG('text',{x:W-padR-10,y:yCrit-6,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':12,fill:'var(--cyan)'});
      t.textContent = `kp = n (= ${n})`;
      svg.appendChild(t);
    }

    // shade subcritical (below) and supercritical (above)
    if(yCrit > padT){
      svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{
        x:padL, y:yCrit, width:W-padR-padL, height:H-padB-yCrit,
        fill:'var(--blue)','fill-opacity':0.07, stroke:'none'}));
    }
    if(yCrit < H-padB){
      svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{
        x:padL, y:padT, width:W-padR-padL, height:yCrit-padT,
        fill:'var(--green)','fill-opacity':0.07, stroke:'none'}));
    }

    // the chosen point (k, p)
    const kp = k*p;
    const px = sx(p), py = sy(Math.min(7.5, kp));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:px,cy:py,r:7,fill:'var(--yellow)',stroke:'#111','stroke-width':1.5}));

    // labels for the regions
    const tHigh = SVG('text',{x:W-padR-10,y:padT+18,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':12,fill:'var(--green)','font-weight':600});
    tHigh.textContent = "supercritical: ↪ Hölder C^{0,γ}";
    svg.appendChild(tHigh);
    const tLow = SVG('text',{x:W-padR-10,y:H-padB-10,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':12,fill:'var(--blue)','font-weight':600});
    tLow.textContent = "subcritical: ↪ L^{p*}";
    svg.appendChild(tLow);

    // verdict text
    let regime, detail;
    if(Math.abs(kp - n) < 1e-6){
      regime = "critical";
      detail = `kp = n = ${n}.  Embeds into every L^q (q < ∞), but fails to reach L^∞.`;
    } else if(kp < n){
      const pstar = (n*p)/(n - k*p);
      regime = "subcritical";
      detail = `kp = ${kp.toFixed(2)} < n = ${n}.  W^{k,p} ↪ L^{p*} with p* = np/(n−kp) = ${pstar.toFixed(2)}.`;
    } else {
      regime = "supercritical";
      const gamma = k - n/p;
      detail = `kp = ${kp.toFixed(2)} > n = ${n}.  W^{k,p} ↪ C^{0,γ} with γ = k − n/p = ${gamma.toFixed(2)} (mod 1).`;
    }
    out.textContent = `(n, k, p) = (${n}, ${k}, ${p.toFixed(1)}) → regime: ${regime}.\n${detail}`;
  }
  for(const el of [nS,kS,pS]) el.addEventListener('input', render);
  render();
})();
</script>

7. Connections

PDEs are the engine of modern analysis — every advance in regularity, weak solutions, or function spaces has fed back into the rest of mathematics.

Functional analysis and Sobolev spaces

Functional analysis + Sobolev spaces supply the weak-solution machinery: Lax–Milgram, Riesz representation, compact embeddings. Galerkin approximation reduces existence to finite-dimensional linear algebra.

Harmonic functions and elliptic regularity

Harmonic-function theory is the prototype; maximum principles and Harnack inequalities transfer (with technical work) to general elliptic operators in non-divergence form.

Stochastic calculus and Feynman–Kac

Stochastic calculus and Brownian motion give probabilistic representations of solutions to parabolic PDEs; conversely, PDE methods analyse SDEs.

Differential geometry

Curvature flows (Ricci, mean-curvature), heat-equation methods (Bochner, Bismut), and the analytic side of Riemannian geometry all run through PDE theory.

Open frontiers: regularity for nonlinear elliptic / parabolic systems (de Giorgi–Nash–Moser refinements); free-boundary problems; rough paths and stochastic PDEs; geometric analysis program (Cheeger–Colding, Perelman); gradient flows in metric spaces.
